curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: I remember the first time I heard a Rhodes piano through a Leslie cabinet — I was maybe nineteen, sitting on the floor of this tiny studio in Muscle Shoals, and I just started crying. Not sad crying. The other kind. I didn't know a machine could breathe like that.
quotes.speaker: Bonnie Raitt
quotes.context: Interview with American Songwriter magazine, published in their March/April 2019 print edition
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Bonnie's deep reverence for the instruments and studios of that era is completely documented, and the emotional specificity reads like her. The subtle giveaway is 'I didn't know a machine could breathe like that,' which is just a hair too poetic for off-the-cuff speech.

quotes.text: Gluttony and lust – y'know, Caligula vibes! What else is there? I've been slothful, definitely. I've ticked the wrath, greed and heresy boxes too.
quotes.speaker: Hozier
quotes.context: In a Hotpress interview about his concept album Unreal Unearth, asked which of the seven deadly sins were the most fun to commit.

source.title: Hotpress — 12 Interviews of Xmas: Hozier on Irishness, Deadly Sins, Joni Mitchell and Unreal Unearth

quotes.text: This whole past year has been some of the worst moments of my life, but also some moments of transcendental beauty and gratitude for the blessings I have in my life
quotes.speaker: James Ford
quotes.context: Discussing writing his new album while receiving treatment for leukemia

source.title: Music — James Ford tells us about writing new album ‘Lost In Another World’ while in hos

quotes.text: There's a reason every great soul record has a moment where the arrangement drops out and it's just the voice and maybe a kick drum. It's not a production trick. Somebody along the way just understood that sometimes you have to clear the room so the feeling has somewhere to go.
quotes.speaker: D'Angelo
quotes.context: Rare extended interview with MOJO magazine, published in their collector's edition soul special, February 2016
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — D'Angelo's intense focus on the interplay between space and emotion in soul production is thoroughly documented, and the specific detail about the kick drum gives it the feel of a producer thinking out loud. The tell is that the idea is expressed slightly too fluently for a notoriously guarded interviewee.

quotes.text: People keep asking if I'm worried about the album leaking and honestly — yeah, a little, but mostly I'm terrified someone's gonna hear the demo vocals I left on track seven by accident and think that's intentional.
quotes.speaker: Jack Antonoff
quotes.context: Appearance on the Switched on Pop podcast, episode released October 2022
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Jack talks constantly about the chaos and self-exposure of the production process, and the anxious, self-deprecating humor matches his documented interview persona perfectly. The giveaway is the oddly specific 'track seven,' which is the kind of concrete detail a real speaker would leave vague.

quotes.text: I think, actually, I'd be more successful if I was OK wearing a muzzle.
quotes.speaker: Chappell Roan
quotes.context: In a January 2025 BBC interview after being named BBC Sound of 2025, on how her outspokenness affects her career.

source.title: The Hollywood Reporter — Chappell Roan: "I'd Be More Successful If I Was OK Wearing a Muzzle"
